report.title: Annual commitments report
report.body: Every January we publish a full audit of the five commitments: metrics, programs that worked, programs that did not.

index.metaDescription: Blacknel's five public commitments: professional development, diversity and inclusion, sustainability, social impact, and work-life balance. Audited every year.

professional-development.title: Professional development and training
professional-development.subtitle: Investing in our team's technical and professional growth — with budget, protected time, and mentorship — is the foundation for everything else.
professional-development.metaTitle: Professional development and training · Blacknel Commitments
professional-development.metaDescription: How Blacknel invests in its team's growth: monthly learning budget, 1:1 mentorship, Individual Development Plans, and cross-functional rotations.
description: At Blacknel we believe people grow when given autonomy, a clear path, and real time to learn. It's not a perk — it's part of the contract.

Every team member has an Individual Development Plan (IDP) reviewed quarterly, a monthly learning budget that needs no pre-approval, and at least 10% of their weekly time protected for applied learning.

principles.title: Time, not just money
principles.body: Anyone can pay for a course. Few companies protect the time to finish it. We do.

principles.title: Dual track: IC or manager
principles.body: Senior progression doesn't require managing people. We have parallel tracks with equivalent compensation.

principles.title: Applied learning
principles.body: What you learn ships in the next sprint or gets taught to the team. Knowledge that doesn't circulate doesn't matter.

programs.title: Monthly learning budget
programs.body: $150 USD/month per person for courses, books, subscriptions, and conferences — no pre-approval. Spend it on what you need.

programs.title: Individual Development Plan (IDP)
programs.body: Reviewed every quarter with your manager. Technical goals, soft skills, next role, and a concrete plan to get there.

programs.title: Internal 1:1 mentorship
programs.body: Bi-annual pairing program: every senior IC mentors a junior; every new manager gets an internal coach.

programs.title: Conference sponsorship
programs.body: We cover ticket + travel for one conference per year (Next.js Conf, React Summit, AWS re:Invent, Customer.io).

programs.title: Friday Tech Talks
programs.body: Internal 45-minute slot every Friday for anyone to present something they learned. Recorded and archived.

programs.title: Cross-functional rotations
programs.body: After 18 months in a role, you can request a 3-month rotation to another area (eng ↔ design ↔ product).

professional-development.accountability: Owner: People Team. Public audit published every January.

diversity-equity-inclusion.title: Diversity, equity, and inclusion
diversity-equity-inclusion.subtitle: We build for diverse audiences across LATAM and the US. Our team should look like them — not like the industry.
diversity-equity-inclusion.metaTitle: Diversity, equity, and inclusion · Blacknel Commitments
diversity-equity-inclusion.metaDescription: Blacknel's commitment to DEI: structured hiring, public salary bands, gender parity targets, independent annual audit, and LATAM sponsorship programs.
description: Building software for multiple markets, languages, and cultures requires a team that shares that plurality. Diversity isn't a marketing KPI — it's a technical condition of the product.
We apply the same practices we expect from our customers: structured hiring, transparent compensation bands, accessibility by default, and public annual accountability.

principles.title: Structural equity, not good intentions
principles.body: Bias is mitigated by process — rubrics, panels, data — not statements.

principles.title: Public compensation
principles.body: Internal salary bands visible to the entire team. Same level + same location = same compensation.

principles.title: Accessibility by default
principles.body: Product and this site meet WCAG 2.2 AA. Annual audit with AccessLab.

programs.title: Structured hiring
programs.body: Published interview rubrics, diverse panels (≥ 1 underrepresented gender), anonymous CV screen on first round.

programs.title: Public salary bands
programs.body: Bands defined by level + location, reviewed every 6 months against benchmarks (Levels.fyi · Honeit LATAM).

programs.title: Gender parity — 2027 target
programs.body: Today: 38% women on the full team, 31% in technical roles. Public target: 50% / 45% by Q4 2027.

programs.title: Annual DEI audit
programs.body: Independent third party audits compensation, retention, and promotion by gender, origin, and age. Published every January.

programs.title: LATAM sponsorship
programs.body: Financial sponsorship and mentorship for PionerasDev (Colombia), Geek Girls LatAm (Mexico), Laboratoria, and MujeresIT.

programs.title: Pronouns optional, respected always
programs.body: Pronouns optional on profiles, signatures, and Slack. Respected always. Never required.

diversity-equity-inclusion.accountability: Owner: People Team + DEI Committee. External audit published every January.

sustainability.title: Sustainability
sustainability.subtitle: Software has a carbon footprint. We measure it, reduce it, and offset the rest. Carbon-neutral since 2025. Carbon-negative by 2028.
sustainability.metaTitle: Sustainability and carbon footprint · Blacknel Commitments
sustainability.metaDescription: Blacknel's sustainability commitment: 100% renewable cloud regions, green-by-design code standards, annual GHG audit, and verified reforestation in the Monarch Butterfly corridor.
description: Every function that runs, every image that's served, every GB transferred generates emissions. Most SaaS companies don't measure them. We do.
We've published an annual carbon footprint balance since 2024. In 2025 we reached carbon-neutral. The public goal is carbon-negative by 2028 — absorbing more than we emit.

principles.title: What isn't measured doesn't exist
principles.body: Every serverless function has a compute budget. Every deploy publishes its emissions delta.

principles.title: Reduce first, offset second
principles.body: Offsetting is the last resort. First: efficient architecture, renewable regions, optimized code.

principles.title: Radical transparency
principles.body: Annual footprint report published with open methodology and third-party verification.

programs.title: 100% renewable cloud regions
programs.body: Vercel (Cloudflare-powered) · AWS us-east-1 and eu-west-1 with contractual renewables. We don't operate in non-renewable regions.

programs.title: Green coding standards
programs.body: Per-endpoint cold-start budgets, mandatory image optimization (AVIF/WebP), lazy-loading by default, ISR over SSR where applicable.

programs.title: Annual footprint audit
programs.body: Calculated with GHG Protocol + Cloud Carbon Footprint. Verified by Climate Neutral Group every January.

programs.title: Verified offsets
programs.body: We offset 110% of residual via Pacto por el Bosque (Mexico): reforestation in the Monarch Butterfly corridor. Verra-verified.

programs.title: Remote-first → less commuting
programs.body: 100% remote cuts commuting emissions by ~70% vs. in-office. Per-capita calculation published.

programs.title: Hardware lifecycle
programs.body: Refresh every 4 years (not 2). Equipment refurbished and resold via certified partner.

sustainability.accountability: Owner: VP Engineering + Operations. Independent audit by Climate Neutral Group every January.

social-impact.title: Social impact
social-impact.subtitle: The 1·1·1·1 commitment: 1% of revenue, 1% of equity, 1% of product, and 1% of team time — all dedicated to social initiatives across LATAM and the US.
social-impact.metaDescription: Blacknel's 1·1·1·1 pledge: 1% of revenue, equity, product, and team time dedicated to social impact. Partners: Techo, Endeavor LATAM, Ashoka Mexico, NetHope.
description: We exist to amplify brands, but our deeper mission is to amplify communities. We've adopted the Pledge 1% model extended to 1·1·1·1 — a commitment embedded in financial reporting and product.
Every quarter we publish how much was donated, to whom, in which currency, and with what outcome. This isn't marketing CSR — it's audited by the same team that audits the financials.

principles.title: Structural, not charitable
principles.body: 1·1·1·1 is committed in the company bylaws. It doesn't depend on the CEO's will or a good fiscal year.

principles.title: LATAM focus
principles.body: 70% of the allocation goes to initiatives operating in Mexico, Colombia, Chile, Peru, and Argentina.

principles.title: Quarterly report
principles.body: Published the first month of each quarter. Audited by external finance.

programs.title: Blacknel 1·1·1·1 pledge
programs.body: 1% of annual revenue donated to verified partners: Techo, Endeavor LATAM, Ashoka Mexico, Pro Bono Network.

programs.title: Free product for nonprofits
programs.body: Free Growth plan for 100+ verified nonprofits across LATAM and the US. Apply at blacknel.com/commitments/social-impact.

programs.title: Brands for Good
programs.body: 5 nonprofits per year receive full pro-bono management of their digital presence (publishing, inbox, reports).

programs.title: Paid volunteer day
programs.body: Each team member gets 1 paid day per quarter to volunteer for a cause of their choice.

programs.title: Equity pledge
programs.body: 1% of the initial cap-table reserved for Pledge 1% International. On exit, materializes as an impact endowment.

programs.title: Active partnerships
programs.body: Techo · Endeavor LATAM · Ashoka Mexico · NetHope · TechSoup LATAM · PionerasDev.

social-impact.accountability: Owner: COO + Pledge Committee. Quarterly audit, joint internal finance + external.

work-life-balance.title: Work-life balance
work-life-balance.subtitle: Work fits life — not the other way around. Remote-first, async-by-default, we measure outcomes not hours. Mental health is not a perk.
work-life-balance.metaTitle: Work-life balance and wellbeing · Blacknel Commitments
work-life-balance.metaDescription: How Blacknel builds a sustainable work environment: remote-first, async-by-default, unlimited PTO with a 25-day minimum, 16-week parental leave, and sabbatical every 4 years.
description: Work-life balance is not flex hours — it's a complete system that respects life outside of work. That means operating decisions, not LinkedIn slogans.
We operate async-by-default, with a maximum of 2 meetings/day and meeting-free Wednesdays. PTO has a minimum (not a maximum) because minimums protect the people who don't dare take time off.

principles.title: Outcomes, not hours
principles.body: No hour tracking. Every role has explicit quarterly OKRs. If you hit them, we don't care when you worked.

principles.title: Async by default
principles.body: Every important decision is documented. If a meeting was required, it's recorded and summarized in writing.

principles.title: Mental health on par with physical
principles.body: Monthly stipend for therapy, meditation apps, or coaching. No justification, no receipts.

programs.title: Remote-first global
programs.body: 100% remote. No mandatory core hours. Any timezone between UTC-8 and UTC+2.

programs.title: Unlimited PTO · 25-day minimum
programs.body: Yes, unlimited. But also a public minimum of 25 days/year your manager must ensure you take.

programs.title: Parental leave · 16 weeks
programs.body: 16 weeks paid at 100% for anyone becoming a parent — adoption included, no gender distinction.

programs.title: No-meeting Wednesdays
programs.body: Full day blocked on team calendars. Exceptions require explicit CEO approval.

programs.title: Wellbeing stipend
programs.body: $100 USD/month for therapy, meditation, gym, or coaching. No justification, no receipts.

programs.title: Sabbatical · every 4 years
programs.body: 4 extra paid weeks every 4 continuous years at the company. Stackable with regular PTO.

programs.title: 4-day workweek pilot
programs.body: Q3 2026: 6-month pilot with 80% of the org. Same salary, Fridays off. Results published at close.

work-life-balance.accountability: Owner: People Team + Founders. Quarterly burnout survey with aggregated data published.

step1.title: Connect your channels
step1.description: Plug in Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, Google Business, and 20+ sources via official OAuth.

step2.title: Invite your team

step3.title: Operate with AI alongside

social.title: The standard for agencies and chains in LATAM
social.logos: Agencies, franchises, and DTC brands choose Blacknel

1.quote: We moved 12 brands from 4 tools to Blacknel. The team got back 15 hours a week and our clients can feel it.
1.author: María Hernández

2.quote: The AI suggestions respect our brand voice better than any assistant we’ve tried. Our response time dropped 62%.
2.author: Diego Aranda

3.quote: We run 48 locations across five countries. Blacknel was the first platform that scaled without fighting our RBAC.
3.author: Sofía Rendón

pricing.title: Pricing without surprises
pricing.description: Three simple plans in USD. 14-day trial on any plan, no credit card.

questions.q: Do I really not need a credit card to try?
questions.a: No. Your 14-day trial starts with just a business email. We'll notify you 3 days before the trial ends, so you can decide whether to subscribe. No auto-charge, no surprises.

questions.q: Do you support official WhatsApp Business?
questions.a: Yes. We connect via Meta's official WhatsApp Business Cloud API. That means you can reply, automate, and report on WhatsApp with the same enterprise-grade compliance any major brand uses. If your number isn't on WhatsApp Business yet, we help you onboard.

questions.q: Can my agency manage brands across many clients?
questions.a: Yes. Blacknel is built for agencies first. Each client lives as an isolated brand with its own connected accounts, inbox, and reports. The Growth plan includes 10 brands. Enterprise is unlimited. Agencies can filter dashboards per brand or consolidate multi-client operations from a single command center.

questions.q: How do you handle compliance in Mexico?
questions.a: We comply with LFPDPPP (Mexico), GDPR (EU), and CCPA (California). Our standard contract includes a signable DPA and SCCs (standard contractual clauses) when clients require international data transfer. SOC 2 Type II is in active audit with target close in Q3 2026.

questions.q: Do you have a public API?
questions.a: REST API and Webhooks are included with the Enterprise plan. For Growth and Standard, we expose integrations via Zapier, Make, and n8n. Our public TypeScript and Python SDK is in private beta — if you need access before Q4 2026, reach out to sales@blacknel.com.

cta.title: Your first week without Hootsuite. Without Sprout. Without the 5 spreadsheets.
cta.description: Connect your channels in 10 minutes, invite your team, and start operating. No contracts, no mandatory sales calls.

techStack.title: Built on industry-leading tech
techStack.description: The same pieces Vercel, Linear, Resend, and thousands of serious teams already trust.

newsletterInline.title: Get the Blacknel playbooks
newsletterInline.description: Once a month. What we learn operating brands across LATAM. No spam, no teasers.

hero.title: One platform for your entire digital operation
hero.subtitle: Inbox, publishing, reputation, AI, ads, and analytics. Each module designed to replace an expensive tool with something better.

inbox.title: Unified inbox
inbox.subtitle: Every channel in a single inbox
inbox.description: Centralize Facebook Messenger, Instagram DM, WhatsApp Business, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, TikTok, Google Business, email, and forms. Route to teams, add internal notes, use saved replies, and track SLAs live.
inbox.lead: Every DM, every new review, every comment, every WhatsApp message, and every inbound email in one place. With smart routing, customer context, and live SLA tracking.
inbox.how: You connect Facebook Messenger, Instagram DM, WhatsApp Business, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, TikTok, Google Business, and your web forms. Blacknel ingests every message into a unified inbox, enriches it with customer history, and applies routing rules by channel, hour, language, or agent skill.
bullets: Auto-routing by rules (channel, hour, language, skill)
SLA tracking with Slack and email alerts
inbox.forWhom: Ideal for customer service teams receiving messages across 4+ channels simultaneously, agencies managing multiple clients, and brands whose support tickets grow 20%+ monthly.

publishing.title: Social publishing
publishing.subtitle: One composer, 10 networks, zero copy-paste
publishing.description: Design a post, pick channels, and let Blacknel adapt format, dimensions, and preview. Approval flows, visual calendar, and drag-to-reschedule.
publishing.lead: You write the post once. Blacknel adapts format, dimensions, copy variations, and faithful previews for every network. With drag-and-drop calendar, multi-level approvals, and scheduled publishing to 10 channels.
publishing.how: The composer detects which channels you select and shows exact previews of how the post will look on Instagram Feed vs Reels vs Stories vs Facebook vs LinkedIn vs Twitter/X. Edit variations per channel or publish identical. Schedule with the visual calendar or publish immediately. If your role requires approval, the post goes to the approvals queue before publishing.
bullets: Supports Feed, Reels, Stories, Shorts, and Threads
Brand voice consistency check via AI before publishing
Bulk import from Google Sheets or CSV
Optimal posting time (AI suggestion of best time to post)
publishing.forWhom: Agencies publishing 50+ posts/week, multi-country brands needing legal approvals per market, and in-house teams that want to kill copy-paste between Buffer/Hootsuite/Notion.

reviews.title: Reviews & reputation
reviews.subtitle: Every review in one panel. Every crisis, caught early.
reviews.description: Centralize Google Business, Yelp, TripAdvisor, Trustpilot, Facebook, and app stores. Reply with templates or AI. Detect sentiment drops before they turn into a crisis.
reviews.lead: Every review from Google, Yelp, TripAdvisor, App Store, and more in one dashboard. With AI that suggests responses in your tone and crisis detection that warns you before problems explode.
reviews.how: We connect Google Business Profile, Yelp, TripAdvisor, Trustpilot, Facebook Reviews, Apple App Store, and Google Play. Every new review enters the inbox with sentiment analysis, AI response suggestion, and automatic categorization. The anomaly detection engine monitors sentiment shifts and triggers alerts when it sees crisis patterns (30%+ rating drop in 7 days, negative mention spikes, etc.).
bullets: AI replies aligned to brand voice
NPS embedded in site and email
reviews.forWhom: Multi-location chains with 10+ locations, restaurants and hotels that live or die by reviews, and DTC brands with App Store presence.

ai.title: Native AI
ai.subtitle: Copilots trained on your voice and your rules
ai.description: This isn’t ChatGPT in a wrapper. Blacknel trains on your brand voice docs, your history, and your previous responses. Generate copy, suggest replies, detect crises.
ai.lead: It's not ChatGPT wrapped. Blacknel trains on your brand voice docs, your post history, your previous replies, and your business rules. The AI writing on your brand's behalf reads your brand before writing.
ai.how: You upload your brand voice doc (PDF, Notion, Google Doc) or import directly from Brand Hub. Blacknel builds a vector brand profile including tone, forbidden vocabulary, representative examples, and compliance rules. When AI generates copy, replies to a DM, or suggests optimizations, it does so ANCHORED to that brand profile — not to a generic model. Powered by Anthropic Claude (default) + OpenAI GPT (fallback).
bullets: Copy generation in ES, EN, PT (other languages on roadmap)
Contextual suggestions in inbox (3 variants per tone)
Ad optimization recommendations with explicit reasoning
Automatic crisis detection via sentiment anomaly
Translation matching brand tone (not just Google Translate)
Powered by Claude Sonnet 4.6 + GPT-4.1 + fallback strategy
ai.forWhom: Teams that already tried 'GPT in the inbox' and got frustrated because it doesn't respect tone. Brands with strict compliance that need AI with real guardrails.

ads.title: Ads management
ads.subtitle: Media buying with the friction it deserves — zero
ads.description: Connect Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, and TikTok. Track spend, ROAS, and CPA cross-channel. Receive actionable alerts and apply recommendations with one click.
ads.lead: Your media buying with the friction it deserves — zero. Connect Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, and TikTok. Track spend, ROAS, and CPA cross-channel without opening 4 dashboards. Receive actionable alerts and apply AI recommendations with human approval.
ads.how: You connect your ad accounts via official OAuth. Blacknel syncs spend, conversions, ROAS, and CPA hourly. If a campaign spikes CPA above threshold, or a budget will burn before scheduled, you get an alert in Slack/email with the reason and recommendation. Optimization recommendations (pause adset, move budget, expand audience) require human approval to execute — nothing happens automatically with your money.
bullets: Unified dashboard of spend and conversions
Budget overrun, CPA spike, CTR drop alerts
ads.forWhom: Agencies managing $50K+ USD/month in spend, DTC performance marketers, and CMOs who want to understand ROAS without asking their agency for an export.

analytics.title: Analytics & reporting
analytics.subtitle: Data executives understand and agencies present
analytics.description: Custom dashboards, competitor benchmarks, scheduled PDF/CSV reports, and exports to Slack or email. White label for agencies.
analytics.lead: Data executives understand and agencies present. Custom drag-and-drop dashboards, industry-benchmarked competitor comparisons, and scheduled PDF/CSV reports with white label for agencies.
analytics.how: Each brand has its analytics workspace with predefined dashboards (Social, Inbox SLA, Reputation, Ads) and the option to build custom dashboards with drag-and-drop. You set recurring reports (daily, weekly, monthly) that get sent as PDF to your inbox or your client's, or published to a Slack channel. Full white label: your logo, your colors, your pre-rendered annotations.
bullets: Industry benchmarks (food, retail, hotels, agencies, etc.)
Export to Excel, CSV, Google Sheets
Full white label (logo, colors, custom domain)
Compare periods (this month vs last vs YoY)
Public share links with optional password
analytics.forWhom: Agencies sending monthly reports to 10+ clients (this feature alone saves you 8 hours/month), CMOs needing board-ready reports, and multi-location operations with cross-location KPIs.

listening.title: Listening & competitors
listening.subtitle: What people say about you — and about your competition
listening.description: Monitor mentions, keywords, hashtags, and competitors. Share of voice, sentiment trends, and real-time alerts.
listening.lead: What they're saying about you — and your competition — in real time. Mention monitoring, keywords, hashtags, and competitors with share of voice, sentiment trends, and actionable alerts.
listening.how: You define keywords (your brand, products, competitors, industry). Blacknel monitors Twitter/X, Reddit, news, blogs, forums, and Google Trends hourly. Each mention is classified by sentiment and clustered into topics. Share of voice shows how you compare to competitors. Real-time alerts when mention volume exceeds thresholds or new topics appear.
bullets: Raw data export for external analysis
listening.forWhom: Brands with reputation to defend, PR teams at mid/large companies, and growth marketers who want to understand narrative vs competitors.

compareTable.title: Compare against tools you already know

compareTable.footnote: Comparison based on public pricing as of January 2026. Some Hootsuite/Sprout features available only in Enterprise tiers.

standard.name: Standard
standard.description: For SMBs and independent operators stepping out of the chaos.

growth.name: Growth
growth.description: For growing businesses and small agencies that need to scale without fighting the tool.

enterprise.name: Enterprise
enterprise.description: For large enterprises, enterprise agencies, and multi-location chains with serious compliance.

comparison.title: Detailed comparison
comparison.subtitle: Same product, different limits. No catches.

questions.a: If your company is in Mexico, yes — we charge 16% IVA on the subscription and issue a CFDI 4.0 electronic invoice. If you're outside Mexico, the USD prices shown are final, with no additional VAT.
Yes, anytime. Upgrades take effect immediately with prorated billing. Downgrades apply to the next billing cycle. No penalty for changing plans, no mandatory annual contract.

questions.q: Do you charge per extra user?
questions.a: Only if you exceed your plan's cap. Standard includes 1 user, Growth 5 users, Enterprise unlimited. To add users on Standard or Growth, additional seats are $19 USD/month each. Enterprise has no per-seat charge.
Yes. Paying 12 months upfront gets you 20% off on any plan. For multi-year deals (24+ months) on Enterprise tickets, contact us for custom terms.

questions.q: Do you charge a setup fee?
questions.a: Standard and Growth: zero setup fee. Enterprise: dedicated onboarding with a Customer Success Manager included at no extra cost. We don't charge setup fees regardless of deal size — we'd rather the customer's success be the ROI.

noChargeExtra.title: What we DON'T charge extra for

items.title: Onboarding and setup
items.body: Included in every plan, no matter your team size.

items.title: Migration from Hootsuite/Sprout/Birdeye
items.body: We help you move your brands at no extra cost.

items.title: Chat and email support
items.body: Included in every plan. No gating to the highest tier.

items.title: Product updates
items.body: Automatic, no upgrade fees, no per-version licensing.

items.title: New connectors when launched
items.body: Every new integration ships without an upcharge.

items.title: Asset storage
items.body: 50GB on Standard, 500GB on Growth, unlimited on Enterprise.

hero.title: We’re building the digital presence modern brands deserve
hero.subtitle: Blacknel started in Mexico City with a simple mission: operating many brands across many channels shouldn’t feel like carrying water in buckets.

mission.title: Mission
paragraphs: We give every agency, chain, and brand a single brain to operate their digital presence — regardless of how many networks they use, how many brands they run, or how many countries they cover.

We don't believe in tools that charge you for every new feature. We believe in platforms that scale with you from 3 brands to 300, with the same transparent pricing, the same support, and the same UX quality.

The AI we put in your team respects two things: your brand voice and your autonomy. We suggest, but we don't decide for you. We detect crises, but we don't act without your approval. We optimize your ads, but we never move money without your OK.
If after a year using us you can't imagine going back to Hootsuite, Sprout, or the 5 spreadsheets, we did our job right.

vision.title: Vision
paragraphs: In 2030, managing the multi-channel digital presence of a brand should be as natural as sending an email. No parallel spreadsheets, no ten logins per team, no abusive enterprise contracts, no 'connect your developer to see the KPIs'.
We get to that vision by building every feature as a brick in a larger system, not as an isolated unit sold standalone. Every part of Blacknel exists because the others exist.
By 2030, we want that when someone starts a marketing agency, a franchise, or a DTC brand, the question 'which tool do we use' has an obvious answer.

story.title: Why we exist
paragraphs: After watching an agency team lose 30 hours a week pasting content between Hootsuite, Sprout, and Birdeye, we decided it was absurd.
Blacknel is the tool we wanted for ourselves — and the one we believe any serious team deserves to have without justifying TCO to a procurement committee.
story.quote: We built the tool we wanted for ourselves after watching agency teams lose 30 hours a week to copy-paste between five platforms.

items.title: Product over vanity
items.body: We don’t announce what we don’t ship. Every release has measurable impact.

items.title: Multi-tenant for real
items.body: Data isolation isn’t a sticker. It’s our baseline architecture.

items.title: Pricing without surprises
items.body: What you see is what you pay. No forced contracts, no hidden paywalls.

items.title: AI with humans in the loop
items.body: Our AI suggests. Your team decides. Never the other way around.

decisions.title: How we decide
decisions.subtitle: The five criteria that order every product, pricing, and business decision.

items.title: Product over hype
items.body: We don't announce what we don't ship. Every feature in our changelog is in production for real customers. No abusive 'coming soon' in our own copy.

items.title: Customer pain > Feature wishlist
items.body: We build features when 3+ customers ask for the same thing or when we see a usage pattern indicating a gap. We don't build because 'it would look good in a demo'.

items.title: Migration > Lock-in
items.body: If you want to leave Blacknel, you export all your data as CSV/JSON without contacting support. If you choose us, we want it to be because we serve you, not because it's costly to leave.

items.title: Pricing transparent > Hidden fees
items.body: What you see on /pricing is what you pay. No 'contact sales for pricing' on public plans, no surprise upcharges, no SLA buried in a contractual oddity.

items.title: AI respects boundaries
items.body: Our AI has three rules: don't invent facts, don't act with money without approval, don't write anything that contradicts your brand voice doc. If we break any of these, it's a critical bug.

team.title: Team
team.subtitle: We're hiring for 3 roles. If you think you fit, write us.

founder.name: Carlos Anaya Ruiz
bioParagraphs: Engineer and operator with experience building SEO, paid media, and analytics teams for multi-location brands across LATAM and the United States. Before founding Blacknel, he led digital marketing at firms with a presence in 10+ locations.
He lived the problem Blacknel solves: 30 hours a week lost pasting content between Hootsuite, Sprout, and Google Sheets. Based in Mexico City with his two dogs.

hero.title: Playbooks, research, and field notes
hero.subtitle: What we learn running brands across LATAM and the US. Unfiltered.

hero.title: Real teams operating with Blacknel
hero.subtitle: Agencies, chains, and DTC brands replacing three tools with one.

studioNorte.background: Studio Norte is a boutique agency in Mexico City with 12 active client accounts ranging from DTC food brands to boutique hotels. Their 8-person team operated publishing, inbox, and reviews in 4 different tools (Hootsuite for publishing, ManyChat for inbox, Birdeye for reviews, and Google Sheets for reporting).
studioNorte.challenge: 12 brands distributed across 4 tools, zero cross-client visibility, and a team exhausted from switching tabs 30 times a day. Monthly reports took 8 hours per client. Inbox responses took 3+ hours because they depended on who was 'inside' the correct tool.
studioNorte.solution: They migrated all 12 brands to Blacknel in two weeks — using the migration assistant that imports directly from Hootsuite. They activated multi-level approval workflows and white-label dashboards with Studio Norte branding, so end clients saw reports without knowing they ran on Blacknel.
studioNorte.quote: The team recovered time now spent on client strategy, not operations. And clients can feel the difference in response speed and monthly report quality.

cafeteriaNube.industry: DTC food brand · Mexico and US
cafeteriaNube.background: Cafetería Nube is a DTC specialty coffee brand born in Mexico City that expanded to the US in 2024. They sell a monthly subscription of ground coffee plus an e-commerce gear store. Marketing team of 4 operating 6 channels across 2 countries and 2 languages.
cafeteriaNube.challenge: Inbox flooded with DMs across six channels (IG, FB, WhatsApp, Twitter, TikTok comments, contact form), with response times above 4 hours at campaign peaks. The brand voice — casual but careful, with subtle humor — was lost every time they hired a new agent who hadn't read the brand voice doc.
cafeteriaNube.solution: They centralized messages in Blacknel inbox, enabled AI suggestions with brand voice training (uploaded their 12-page brand book), and configured auto-assign rules by language (ES → MX team, EN → US team) and shift (morning / afternoon / evening).
cafeteriaNube.quote: We tried raw GPT, ChatGPT with custom prompts, another more expensive 'AI sidekick'. None respected our tone. Blacknel does. The team now trusts the suggestions instead of rewriting them.

grupoLoma.background: Grupo Loma operates 48 locations across Mexico, Colombia, Peru, Chile, and Argentina, all under the same brand but with local digital presence: each location has its own Instagram, Facebook, and Google Business Profile. The 5-person central digital team must coordinate publishing and reputation with local marketing managers in each country.
grupoLoma.challenge: 48 locations running reviews independently, no brand governance, no central control over what gets posted, and no visibility into what was happening in each country. Negative reviews piled up because each location had to log into Google Business manually. Local crises were discovered weeks late.
grupoLoma.solution: They rolled out Blacknel with granular RBAC: 'Country Manager', 'Local Marketer', 'Brand Guardian' roles with differentiated permissions. Custom dashboards per country. Global crisis detection with per-location thresholds. Response templates by language and review type (1★, 5★, specific complaint).
grupoLoma.quote: We used to spend 60% of the central team's time just coordinating. Now we spend 60% of the time on strategy and local managers execute within their permissions without asking for approval on every action.

hero.title: Privacy Policy

hero.title: Terms of Service
hero.subtitle: Effective January 1, 2026. By using Blacknel you agree to these terms.

hero.title: Cookie Policy
hero.subtitle: How we use cookies and similar technologies on blacknel.com and the application.

banner.title: We use functional cookies
banner.body: To keep your session and remember preferences. We don’t use tracking cookies. Analytics is Plausible (cookieless, GDPR-friendly).

hero.title: Data Processing Agreement (DPA)
hero.subtitle: For EU customers and operations that require a DPA under GDPR Article 28.

hero.title: Your data, treated like ours
hero.subtitle: End-to-end encryption, strict multi-tenancy, granular RBAC, and full auditing. LFPDPPP, GDPR, and CCPA compliant.

sections.title: Encryption at rest and in transit
sections.lead: Every piece of data Blacknel touches travels and sleeps encrypted. No plaintext, no exceptions.

subsections.title: Data at rest · AES-256
subsections.body: All information stored in Blacknel is encrypted with AES-256 at the Postgres column level. Our database provider (Supabase) adds disk-level encryption as defense-in-depth.

subsections.title: Data in transit · TLS 1.3
subsections.body: All communication between your browser, our servers, and third-party APIs (Facebook, Google, etc.) goes over TLS 1.3 with certificate pinning where the provider supports it. Plain HTTP exists nowhere in the stack.

subsections.title: Secrets and credentials
subsections.body: OAuth credentials (Facebook, Google, etc.) live encrypted in Supabase Vault, with keys that rotate every 90 days. API keys you generate in your workspace are shown once and never stored in plaintext.

subsections.title: Passwords
subsections.body: We don't handle them. We use Supabase Auth with magic links (passwordless) by default. For Enterprise customers with SSO/SAML, the password never reaches Blacknel — your IdP authenticates.

sections.title: Strict multi-tenancy
sections.lead: Each organization is an isolated tenant at the database level. Not a filter applied in code.

subsections.title: Row-Level Security in Postgres
subsections.body: Each organization in Blacknel is an isolated tenant via Postgres Row-Level Security (RLS). It's a database-level constraint that prevents any cross-tenant query, even if there's a bug in the app.

subsections.title: What it means in practice
subsections.body: If a bug in our code tried to execute SELECT * FROM posts without an org filter, Postgres would return 0 rows — because RLS policies verify current_setting('app.current_org_id') before returning any row. Without a valid org_id in the session, there's no data.

subsections.title: Asset isolation
subsections.body: Each org has its own storage bucket for assets (videos, images, PDFs). Buckets are private by default and accessed via signed URLs that expire in 1 hour. There's no way to list or access another org's assets.

subsections.title: Audit trail
subsections.body: Every action touching sensitive data (login, post publish, review response, RBAC change) lands in audit_events with timestamp, user_id, org_id, IP, user-agent, and the diff of what changed. Append-only — history cannot be rewritten.

sections.title: Granular RBAC
sections.lead: Five default roles, custom roles on Enterprise, and three layers of permission checks.

subsections.title: Default roles
subsections.body: Owner (full control + billing), Admin (everything except billing/deletion), Manager (brands and workflows), Agent (day-to-day operations), Viewer (read-only).

subsections.title: Custom roles on Enterprise
subsections.body: Create custom roles with granular permission matrix: per brand, per action (read/write/delete/publish), per channel. Permissions assigned via grants and revokes — the system follows 'revoke wins' on conflict.

subsections.title: Permission catalog
subsections.body: 50+ permission strings available. Examples: posts:publish, inbox:assign, billing:read, audit:read, team:manage_roles.

subsections.title: Defense-in-depth
subsections.body: Every permission check happens in THREE layers: TypeScript in the Server Action, SQL function executing the query, and Row-Level Security at the database. All three would have to fail simultaneously for a permission leak to happen.

sections.title: Immutable audit log
sections.lead: Every action that affects data or configuration is recorded immutably and is exportable.

subsections.title: What gets logged
subsections.body: Logins, logouts, published posts, review replies, role changes, data exports, org settings, pricing/seat changes.

subsections.title: Per-event payload
subsections.body: Exact timestamp (UTC + local), user_id, org_id, session_id, source IP, user-agent, country (GeoIP), exact action + diff (before/after), result (success/failure/partial).

subsections.title: Retention
subsections.body: Standard: 90 days. Growth: 1 year. Enterprise: up to 7 years, configurable.

subsections.title: Immutability and export
subsections.body: The audit_events table is append-only at the Postgres level. No DELETE is allowed — not even with admin privileges. The audit log is exportable as JSON or CSV at any time, useful for SOC 2, ISO 27001, and internal audits.

sections.title: Compliance
sections.lead: LFPDPPP, GDPR, CCPA, and SOC 2 Type II audit in progress.

subsections.title: LFPDPPP (Mexico)
subsections.body: We comply with the principles of legality, consent, information, quality, finality, loyalty, proportionality, and accountability. Privacy notice aligned with INAI. ARCO rights are handled via privacy@blacknel.com within 20 business days.

subsections.title: GDPR (European Union)
subsections.body: We operate under Article 6.1.b (contract execution) and 6.1.a (consent) for marketing opt-in. Designated DPO. Your right to portability (Art. 20) is fulfilled with a full standard-JSON export. SCCs are signed by default for international data transfer.

subsections.title: CCPA (California)
subsections.body: We honor 'Do Not Sell' requests (even though we don't sell data), 'Right to Know' and 'Right to Delete'. Max response time: 45 days.

subsections.title: SOC 2 Type II · in progress
subsections.body: Active audit with target close in Q3 2026. Covers Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy. Once certified, the report will be available to Enterprise customers under NDA.

subsections.title: HIPAA
subsections.body: We don't operate as a Business Associate for healthcare providers by default. If you need specific HIPAA compliance, contact us to discuss a dedicated BAA on Enterprise.

sections.title: Bug bounty
sections.lead: Active private program. If you find a vulnerability, email security@blacknel.com.

subsections.title: What we reward
subsections.body: Critical (RCE, auth bypass, tenant data leak): $1,000 – $5,000 USD. High (privilege escalation, sensitive data exposure): $500 – $1,500 USD. Medium (XSS, CSRF, minor IDOR): $100 – $500 USD. Low (info disclosure, config): $50 – $200 USD.

subsections.title: Rules
subsections.body: Find the vuln with your own account, don't compromise others'. No DoS, brute force, or aggressive scanning. Report privately and give us 90 days before going public. No social engineering of the team.

subsections.title: Hall of fame
subsections.body: We maintain a public Hall of Fame of researchers who reported valid issues. We'll publish it once the first confirmed report lands — full transparency, pre-revenue, no invented history.

architecture.title: Architecture at a glance
architecture.subtitle: Modern, fluid, observable stack. Each layer has a clear responsibility and its own security guardrails.

layers.title: Client
layers.body: Your team accesses via browser or mobile. Everything is HTTPS + TLS 1.3 with HSTS preload.

layers.title: CDN · Cloudflare
layers.body: L3/L4/L7 DDoS protection. Static asset caching. Managed WAF rulesets active.

layers.title: Vercel Edge · Next.js Server Components
layers.body: Global edge rendering. Server Components execute in Vercel Functions with <100ms cold start thanks to Fluid Compute.

layers.title: Server Actions & API Routes
layers.body: Every mutation passes through a triple permission check (TS + SQL + RLS). Auth via Supabase Magic Links. Per-tenant rate limiting.

layers.title: Supabase Postgres · multi-tenant RLS
layers.body: AES-256 encryption at rest, daily backups with PITR (point-in-time recovery), geo-distributed replication.

layers.title: Sub-processors
layers.body: Anthropic (Claude) · OpenAI (GPT) · Resend (email) · Stripe (payments) · Plausible (analytics) · Sentry (errors). Each one has a signed DPA + SCC.

blocks.title: Detection
blocks.body: Sentry alerts on production errors in <1 minute. PostHog tracks usage anomalies. Uptime monitoring 24/7 every 30 seconds. Custom alerts on critical metrics (auth failure rate, DB errors).

blocks.title: Triage
blocks.body: Severity 1 (user-affecting): page on-call <5 min. Severity 2 (partial degradation): response <15 min. Severity 3 (not user-affecting): next business day.

blocks.title: Communication
blocks.body: status.blacknel.com updates in real time. Email to admins of affected orgs within 30 minutes. Public post-mortem on the blog within 7 days for SEV 1.

blocks.title: Learning
blocks.body: Every SEV 1 ends with a documented post-mortem: timeline, root cause, what worked, what didn't, and action items. We publish them because we believe in transparency.

hero.title: Build with a small, serious team
hero.subtitle: Remote-friendly from Mexico City. Real equity. Zero corporate theater.

items.title: Async-first
items.body: Meetings only when needed. We document everything.

items.title: Weekly ship
items.body: Each team ships something every week. Visible in the changelog.

items.title: Real equity
items.body: Stock options vested over 4 years with a 1-year cliff. ISO or NSO depending on country.

items.title: Health and life
items.body: Best private insurance on the Mexican market. Unlimited PTO with a 15-day minimum.

notSomething.title: What we are NOT
notSomething.subtitle: To save time for both sides.

items.title: We are not a rocket-ship 18-month exit
items.body: We are building a durable business. If you want a hyper-growth path with an 18-month exit, there are better places.

items.title: We have no performative perks
items.body: No foosball, no end-of-day beer (unless you want it), no 'we are family' speech.

items.title: We don't do crunch culture
items.body: 40 hours/week is the norm. If you ship in 30, ship in 30. Zero glorification of chronic overtime.

items.title: We don't micromanage
items.body: You know when and how you work best. We expect visible outputs, not time-tracking.

items.title: We are not perfect
items.body: There are processes that hurt, decisions we will reverse, tickets that sit unassigned for months. Honest trumps performative.

engineer.title: Senior Full Stack Engineer
engineer.summary: TypeScript + Next.js + Postgres. You build the heart of the app, the public SDK, and the AI layer. Mentor for 1-2 juniors.
engineer.about: You will build the heart of the Blacknel app: multi-tenant logic, the RBAC system, official connectors with Facebook/Instagram/Google Business, and the AI integration layer. You will also be involved in architecture decisions — not just executing tickets.
stack: Backend: TypeScript + Next.js 16 App Router (Server Actions + API Routes), Postgres with Drizzle ORM, Supabase Auth/Storage
Frontend: same stack — full-stack engineers touch both
AI: Anthropic Claude SDK, OpenAI SDK, prompt engineering
Infra: Vercel deployments, Sentry observability, PostHog analytics, Inngest crons
expectations: 5+ years building complex web applications (not portfolio sites)
Full comfort with TypeScript strict mode
Advanced Postgres: RLS, triggers, window functions, query optimization
API design (REST + Webhooks); experience with OAuth flows
Ideally: experience with AI/LLM integrations (Claude, GPT)
extras: Production experience with Next.js App Router
howWeWork: Ship weekly: every engineer ships something every week visible in the changelog
Mandatory code review for all merges to main
4 intense days, 1 day for deep work / refactor / OSS
benefits: Equity with 4-year vesting (1-year cliff). Senior tier.
Premium private health insurance (Mexico) or equivalent stipend outside MX
$2,000 USD/year stipend for learning (books, courses, conferences)
Macbook Pro M3 or equivalent stipend
process: Initial call with Carlos (45 min) — fit + role context
Technical conversation (90 min) — discuss past projects, no whiteboard
Take-home project (4-8 hrs, paid if completed): multi-tenant endpoint with RLS + tests
Offer · 2-3 weeks from apply to offer

designer.title: Product Designer
designer.summary: End-to-end product design. Figma, code prototyping optional but valued. You will define how Blacknel looks.
designer.about: You will own Blacknel's product design: internal dashboard, marketing site, brand system. This is not a wireframing role — you want to design in high fidelity from day one, connected to engineering with short feedback loops.
stack: Access to the repo and dev environment — reading code and PRs is welcome
Live components: shadcn/ui + Aceternity + Magic UI
Linear for tracking, Notion for docs
expectations: 4+ years designing product in B2B SaaS
Portfolio with shipped products — no concept work
Comfort collaborating with engineers in async context
Strong sense of motion design (Motion / Framer)
extras: Background in data viz / complex dashboards
howWeWork: Async weekly reviews + 1 weekly sync with engineering
Design tokens live in the repo, not in isolated Figma
Ship over polish — but only when something is clear
process: Portfolio walkthrough (60 min) — discuss 3 projects in detail
Design exercise (paid): redesign a real Blacknel flow · 5-10 hrs
Offer · 2-3 weeks from apply to offer

csm.title: Customer Success Manager
csm.summary: Onboarding Growth and Enterprise accounts. Fluent Spanish + English. Prior B2B SaaS experience required.
csm.about: You will own customer success for our first paying accounts. That means onboarding, first-line support for Growth+, identifying expansion opportunities, and the feedback loop with product. You work directly with customers — not behind second-line support.
stack: Metrics: NRR, CSAT, time to first value
expectations: 3+ years in CSM or equivalent roles in B2B SaaS
Fluent Spanish + English — both languages in formal writing
Comfort with metrics and reporting to client executives
Background in agencies or marketing operations is a huge plus
extras: Prior experience with Hootsuite, Sprout, Birdeye, or others (you know which problems your customers migrate)
howWeWork: Every new account has a kickoff doc in Notion
Quarterly Business Reviews for Growth and Enterprise customers
Direct feedback loop with engineering — CSMs file tickets, don't wait
Async-first but available for US biz hours when needed
process: Role-play exercise: simulated onboarding of an agency account
Customer interview (60 min) — interview with an actual design partner
Offer · 2-3 weeks from apply to offer

boilerplate.title: Boilerplate
boilerplate.short: Blacknel is the AI-native platform that brings inbox, publishing, reviews, ads, and analytics into a single control plane. Built for agencies, multi-location brands, and franchises across LATAM and the US.
boilerplate.long: Blacknel is the central nervous system for digital brand operations. The platform unifies inbox, publishing, reviews, and ads — with AI that respects every brand's voice. Built for agencies, multi-location brands, and franchises across LATAM and the US, Blacknel replaces tools like Hootsuite, Sprout Social, and Birdeye in a single workspace. Founded in 2026 by Carlos Anaya Ruiz, the company is based in Mexico City.
